title: Implement Template Inheritance for a Blog Site

source.module: Core Django Fundamentals – Templates & Static Content

topicTags: Core Django Fundamentals – Templates & Static Content

content.problemStatement:
In this worked out example, we'll create base and child templates for a simple blog site. \
**Note:** `base.html` and `posts.html` are 2 separate HTML files in the `/templates/` folder

### How does the code work?
When we run the code - the `/` webpage route loads the file - `base.html`. This loads the header, nav-bar and footer.



When we change the route to `/posts/` - the file `posts.html` is loaded. \
`posts.html` derives the nav-bar and footer from `base.html`. \
`{% block header %}Welcome to My Blog{% endblock %}` also over-rides and replaces the header. \
In addition `{% block content %}` from `posts.html` gets loaded on the webpage.



**Note:** `{% extends "base.html" %}` is required in the 'child' template to ensure that it inherits the properties of its parent.
